name: Family possessions, poor
description: A 3d6 biography table of poor household rights, claims, obligations, and scraps of standing that do not overlap with item rewards.

results.description: A tolerated place to sleep or build, secure only until someone stronger objected.
The right to glean after harvest, worth little in good years and everything in bad ones.
A customary claim to gather fallen wood, reeds, or peat that others disputed whenever scarcity sharpened.
Kin in one nearby place who would take you in briefly, though never without reminding you of the cost.
A household reputation for hard use, low complaint, and payment that came late but did come.
Access to common grazing, shared water, or rough pasture whose value depended on season and patience.
The right to use a village oven, mill, or ferry at older terms than newer families could demand.
A narrow claim to a strip of ground everyone recognized and no one had bothered to define clearly.
A remembered favor owed to the household by someone only slightly better placed than you were.
A place in local custom: not respected exactly, but known, which can matter nearly as much.
The expectation that your household could always be called on first for rough labor and small emergencies.
A family name associated with endurance, thrift, and surviving ugly seasons.
The right to gather what others left behind: stubble, windfall, scraps, or salvage.
Informal access to pathways, crossings, or fishing spots known best by long use rather than law.
A household standing modest enough to be overlooked and solid enough not to be easily pushed aside.
More security than your station should have allowed: customary rights, neighborly obligations, and enough local recognition to make hardship survivable.
